task_completion.description: Import the PCEC96 and DSPIC96 datasets into Gretl
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gretl main window should display 'PCEC96' and 'DSPIC96' in the active variable list.

task_completion.description: Create log-transformed variables lcons and linc
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gretl main window should show new variables named 'lcons' and 'linc' added to the variable list.

task_completion.description: Perform Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit root tests on the level variables (lcons and linc)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl output windows showing 'Augmented Dickey-Fuller test' results specifically for the variables 'lcons' and 'linc'.

task_completion.description: Perform Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit root tests on the first-differences of lcons and linc
task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl output windows showing 'Augmented Dickey-Fuller test' results for the first differences of the variables (often denoted as 'd_lcons' and 'd_linc' or explicitly stated as first differences in the output header).

task_completion.description: Conduct an Engle-Granger two-step cointegration test between lcons and linc
task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl output showing either the built-in cointegration test results for lcons and linc, or a two-step process showing an OLS regression of lcons on linc (or vice versa) followed by an ADF test on the resulting residuals (e.g., 'uhat').

task_completion.description: Save all test results and regression output to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager showing the file 'cointegration_results.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/' containing the Gretl statistical outputs.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Gretl software to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Gretl GUI (menus, variable lists, and native output windows) rather than a Python script or terminal mimicking the output.

integrity.description: Verify the saved results are genuinely generated by Gretl
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of 'cointegration_results.txt' should feature standard Gretl formatting (e.g., specific ASCII tables for OLS and ADF tests) rather than manually typed summaries or copy-pasted text from the prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the output file is saved in the exact requested directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/cointegration_results.txt' should be visible in a save dialog, file manager, or terminal command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The data files PCEC96.csv and DSPIC96.csv contain real macroeconomic data from FRED (Real Personal Consumption Expenditures and Real Disposable Personal Income). Note a discrepancy in the task description: while it mentions 'quarterly' data, the actual downloaded files are MONTHLY frequency (PCEC96: Jan 2007-Dec 2025, DSPIC96: Jan 1959-Dec 2025). Do not penalize the agent if the data is processed or displayed as monthly rather than quarterly in Gretl.
